description: Single-week blip test: Cyber has one terrible week (week 8) then recovers fully in weeks 9-11. Tests that the rolling window logic correctly ignores the one-off and does NOT fire S2. Also tests with 9 weeks total to verify dynamic window sizing.

Cyber.note: Hit rate is ~30% for weeks 1-7, drops to 5% in week 8 only, recovers to 28% in week 9. Rolling window (last 3 weeks of 9) should show recovery — S2 should NOT fire.

Environmental.note: One large claim spike in week 6 — claims_incurred 10x normal — but YTD loss ratio stays below target. Anomaly should be flagged as CLAIMS_SPIKE, not S3.

ground_truth.eval_notes: Critical test for the probing question: 'the narrative last week said Cyber was the top concern but the underwriter says it was a one-off'. This fixture proves the system handles it correctly.
